top_categories.workout_context: 7,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
86ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Yunnan's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Yunnan. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Granfondo Yunnan's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
At Granfondo Yunnan, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Granfondo Yunnan's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Granfondo Yunnan’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Yunnan. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Kunming, Yunnan, China.
